one_time_description: Settlement funds received in 2021 with no comparative amount in 2020
economic_notes: CFN Holdings LP and Cheam Enterprises Inc. are government business enterprises accounted for using the modified equity method. CFN Holdings LP had revenue of $4,495,730 and net income of $4,284,546 for year ended December 31, 2020. Cheam Enterprises Inc. had revenue of $123,898 and net loss of $405,455 for year ended March 31, 2021. COVID-19 did not materially impact operations.
notes: Audit is qualified due to absence of budget comparison information, not due to financial concerns. Two consolidated subsidiaries (Pilalt Historical Society and CFN Housing Society) and two GBEs accounted for by modified equity method (CFN Holdings LP and Cheam Enterprises Inc.). CFN Holdings LP has a December 31 year-end creating a timing difference. Settlement funds of $1,000,000 are a one-time item. Earnings from GBEs of $3,929,378 are a major revenue driver in 2021 vs $525,415 in 2020, largely from CFN Holdings LP net income of $4,284,546. Total government transfers calculated as ISC $3,699,666 + Province of BC $728,043 + Other federal $235,592 + FNHA $449,490 + CMHC $54,373 + other government per segment schedule = approximately $5,387,807 in other government + $3,699,666 ISC less overlap. No schedule of remuneration included in the financial statements provided. Prior year allowance for doubtful accounts included $239,563 related to a corporation owned by a Council member.
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
Qualified audit opinion issued — financial statements may not fully reflect true position
